name: Pico Cervero and Honfrías Trail
name_original: Pico Cervero por el Sendero de las Honfrías – Espacio Natural de las Quilamas
description: Jogging the difficult 11.7-mile Pico Cervero trail offers panoramic views and diverse forests in the Quilamas Natural Space.
region_intro: Jogging routes Linares de Riofrío are set within the mountainous Sierra de Francia-Quilamas-La Calería region, characterized by prominent peaks like Mojón del Marrano and Sierra Chica. The area features diverse natural landscapes, including the mixed woodland of La Honfría forest, home to chestnut and Pyrenean oak trees, along with numerous natural springs. This region is also a protected natural area, offering varied terrain for running. Best jogging routes around Linares De Riofrío The most popular jogging route is Pico Cervero and Honfrías Trail, a difficult 11.7 miles (18.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain and passes through the scenic La Honfría forest. Another top favourite among local runners is Alto del Guijarral – Mirador de Bajenoso loop from San Esteban de la Sierra, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route provides panoramic views from the Mirador de Bajenoso amidst mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Garcibuey – Villanueva del Conde loop from Villanueva del Conde, a 13.9 miles (22.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and villages, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Jogging around Linares De Riofrío is defined by mountainous terrain, dense woodlands like La Honfría forest, and natural springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 runners have used komoot to explore Linares De Riofrío's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Linares De Riofrío?
A: There are 7 dedicated running routes listed in this guide for the Linares De Riofrío area. These routes offer a mix of moderate and difficult options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Linares De Riofrío?
A: While this guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the region's diverse terrain means there are often easier paths available. For example, the "Sendero de las Honfrías" is described as a moderate 7.24 km hike that can be enjoyed as a scenic jog. The area's well-defined paths and manageable elevation changes make it suitable for runners of varying fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Linares De Riofrío?
A: The jogging trails in Linares De Riofrío are characterized by mountainous terrain, dense woodlands like the La Honfría forest, and natural springs. You'll encounter prominent peaks such as Mojón del Marrano and Sierra Chica, offering varied landscapes from challenging ascents to more gentle paths through chestnut and Pyrenean oak trees.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Linares De Riofrío area?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Guijarral – Mirador de Bajenoso loop from San Esteban de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views and a challenging experience.

Q: What are some notable sights or attractions I might see while running in Linares De Riofrío?
A: As you run through Linares De Riofrío, you can discover several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993668124/pico-cervero-and-honfrias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cervero and Honfrías Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through the scenic La Honfría forest. You might also encounter traditional ovens and remnants of an old factory, or enjoy panoramic viewpoints like "El Hueco" and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1188645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervero Peak&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking vistas of the Valle de las Quilamas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Linares De Riofrío?
A: While specific 'easy' routes for families are not highlighted in this guide, the La Honfría forest features recreational areas like Fuente el Cerezo and Fuente de la Honfría. These spots are equipped with picnic areas and playgrounds, making them suitable for a family outing where some members might jog while others enjoy the facilities. Look for routes that pass through these areas for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Linares De Riofrío?
A: The humid climate with abundant springs, streams, and fountains helps keep the environment pleasant even during summer, making it suitable for sustained physical activity. Late winter also brings vibrant displays of daffodils at La Peña Tarrera. The diverse natural landscapes can be enjoyed throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures for running.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging running trails in the region?
A: Yes, Linares De Riofrío offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcibuey – Villanueva del Conde loop from Villanueva del Conde&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.3 km trail with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding workout through varied landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails in Linares De Riofrío?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every route, many trails originate from villages or recreational areas. For example, the "Sendero de las Honfrías" starts from Linares de Riofrío itself, and recreational areas like Fuente el Cerezo and Fuente de la Honfría within La Honfría forest often have facilities that include parking. It's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes for specific parking information.

Q: What makes Linares De Riofrío a unique destination for outdoor running?
A: Linares De Riofrío is unique due to its location within the Sierra de Francia-Quilamas-La Calería region, offering a blend of mountainous terrain, lush mixed woodlands like La Honfría forest, and numerous natural springs. It's also a protected natural area within the Natura 2000 network, ensuring rich biodiversity and stunning panoramic views from points like Pico Cervero, making for a truly immersive running experience.

Q: Are there any less crowded running routes if I prefer solitude?
A: While the guide highlights popular routes, the region's extensive network of trails means you can often find quieter paths, especially away from the main recreational areas. Exploring routes that start from smaller villages or those with higher difficulty ratings, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sequeros&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more solitary experience amidst nature.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in this area?
A: The duration of running routes in Linares De Riofrío varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993668124/pico-cervero-and-honfrias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cervero and Honfrías Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 18.8 km route, typically takes around 2 hours 20 minutes. Shorter, moderate routes would naturally take less time.
tips:
Located between the municipalities of Navarredonda de la Rinconada and Escurial de la Sierra, in the Sierra de Francia-Quilamas region.
A protected natural area within the Natura 2000 network, with oak and chestnut forests and wildlife such as the golden eagle and griffon vulture.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Cervero trail suitable for jogging, given its 'difficult' rating?
faqs.answer: While some sections of the trail, particularly the initial forest tracks, offer smooth surfaces suitable for jogging, the overall route to Pico Cervero is considered difficult. It includes tough switchbacks, steep climbs with bad surfaces, and near the summit, stone stairs and paths between blocks. These challenging sections make continuous jogging demanding, and you'll likely need to walk parts of the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sendero de las Honfrías?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll find well-marked forest tracks and paths with gentle ascents, especially in the lower sections. However, as you gain elevation towards Pico Cervero, the path becomes more challenging, featuring tough climbs, uneven surfaces, and even old cobblestone sections. Near the summit, expect a paved ramp, paths between blocks, and stone stairs.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park and start this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail often starts from Navarredonda de la Rinconada, where you can find suitable parking. The initial parts lead onto a track after a gentle ascent, making it a clear starting point for your adventure.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Cervero trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Quilamas Natural Space, including this trail, is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and the presence of wildlife in this protected area.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to experience the Sendero de las Honfrías?
faqs.answer: The trail offers captivating scenery year-round, but autumn is particularly stunning when the chestnut, oak, and hazelnut forests display a rich array of colors. Spring also brings lush greenery and pleasant temperatures. The Bosque de la Honfría is known as a shady and cool place, making it enjoyable even on warmer days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Quilamas Natural Space?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Quilamas Natural Space or to hike the Sendero de las Honfrías to Pico Cervero. It is a publicly accessible natural area.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the summit of Cervero Peak, the highest point of the Sierra de las Quilamas, offering wide panoramic views of the Sierras of Salamanca and Gredos, Sierra de Francia, Béjar, and the Salmantina plateau. You'll also traverse the beautiful Bosque de la Honfría, known for its centennial chestnut trees and diverse flora. Higher points like Puerto de los Serranos also provide expansive views.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, transitioning from dense forests of chestnut, oak, hazelnut, and holly to sunnier oak groves at higher elevations. The Bosque de la Honfría is a particularly rich botanical area. The route also passes by historical elements like old lime kilns, and the entire area is part of the Natura 2000 network, highlighting its ecological importance.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Quilamas Natural Space?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route often coincides with sections of the GR 182, a long-distance path. It also passes through parts of the Camino de la Pollina and the Linares a La Honfría trail, offering opportunities to explore further within the natural space.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to get refreshments near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, the Bosque de la Honfría section includes recreational areas and a natural spring (Fuente del Cerezo). For more substantial refreshments or amenities, you would typically need to visit nearby villages such as Navarredonda de la Rinconada or Linares de Riofrío before or after your outing.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for making the most of this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and the 'difficult' rating for jogging, consider approaching this route as a challenging trail run with sections of brisk hiking. Focus on enjoying the stunning forest sections for running and be prepared to slow down and appreciate the panoramic views and unique rock formations as you ascend towards Cervero Peak. Starting early can help you avoid warmer temperatures and enjoy the tranquility of the Bosque de la Honfría.
